name: wormhook
description: npm/node supply-chain malware scanner for Claude Code. Tiered hook detecting Shai-Hulud (1.0-3.0 + Mini), the SAP-CAP/AntV/TeamPCP agent-hijack wave, Axios/DPRK RAT, SANDWORM_MODE, the Hades/PyPI .pth startup-hook wave, and remote-eval loaders via filename, SHA256, and behavioral IOCs. Blocks agent-boundary persistence: rogue .claude/.cursor/.continue/.vscode hooks and MCP entries, .vscode/tasks.json folderOpen auto-exec, .pth startup hooks, poisoned git hooks, and LaunchAgent/systemd units. Adds Python (pip/uv) execution gating and a UserPromptSubmit continuous monitor. Ships a wormhook-scan CLI that runs the same engine outside Claude — on-demand fleet scans (git-repo discovery), an hourly local launchd sweep, and a global git hook auditing every git pull — all local, zero-token. Nudges you to pair it with Socket Firewall + safedep/vet for install-time registry defense. At SessionStart emits per-check status lights (🟢/🟡/🔴/⚪) for runtime deps, the ceded install-time firewalls, out-of-band coverage, version drift, and a blast-radius exposure audit that reports long-lived secrets sitting in worm-targeted paths — passphrase-less SSH keys, plaintext GitHub tokens, live-looking .env — so you can shrink the blast radius for when detection misses (advisory only, never blocks). Also ships an action.yml so the same engine runs as a GitHub Action — a required-status-check PR gate (pair with a ruleset that blocks force pushes) that fails the job on a confirmed IOC.
